{{基礎情報 日本の神
| 名 = Isaga-no-Mikoto
| 神祇 =
| 全名 = Isaga-no-Mikoto
| 別名 = Kamu-Isaga-no-Kami, Kushitama-no-Mikoto, Isaka-Miiso-no-Mikoto, {{ill|Kushiyatama-no-Mikoto|ja|クシヤタマ|lt=Kushiyatama-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=櫛八玉命}}
| 別称 =
| 神格 =
| 父 = {{ill|Takehina-no-Mikoto|ja|建比良鳥命|lt=Takehina-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=武雛命}}
| 母 =
| 親 =
| 兄弟姉妹 = {{ill|Izumo-Takeko-no-Mikoto|ja|出雲建子命}}
| 配偶者 =
| 子 = {{ill|Tsusa-no-Mikoto|ja|津狭命}}
| 神社 = Asuki Shrine (Kamu-Isaga Shrine)
| 記紀等 =
| 関連氏族 = {{ill|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造}}
}}
'''Isaga-no-Mikoto''' (''isaga'') is a {{ill|kami|ja|神 (神道)|lt=kami|lt_ja=神}} who appears in the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|出雲国造#系譜|lt=Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko genealogy|lt_ja=出雲国造系図}}.
== Overview ==
In the Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko genealogy, such as the ''Kokuzō {{ill|Kitajima family|ja|出雲国造#北島家|lt=Kitajima family|lt_ja=北島家}} Keifu'' compiled in the early {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, he is recorded as the grandson of {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto|ja|天穂日命}}, the ancestor deity of the {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}}, and the son of Takehina-no-Mikoto (Takehinadori-no-Mikoto); in some transmissions, the 3rd generation is '''Kushitama-no-Mikoto'''{{Sfn|Takashima|1995|pp=175-178}}{{Sfn|Senge|1882}}. There are {{ill|genealogies|ja|系図}} that make Isaga-no-Mikoto an alternate name of Izumo-Takeko-no-Mikoto (Izumotsuhiko){{Sfn|Senge|1882}}, as well as genealogies that make Isaga-no-Mikoto and Izumo-Takeko-no-Mikoto {{ill|brothers|ja|兄弟}} and further transmit Kushiyatama-no-Mikoto as an alternate name of Isaga-no-Mikoto{{Sfn|Shibata|Inamura|1929|p=442}}. In the genealogy of the {{ill|Sugawara clan|ja|菅原氏}}, an alternate name of '''Isaka-Miiso-no-Mikoto''' is transmitted{{Sfn|Mitani|1970|p=20}}. No deity by the same name is found in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' or ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}''.
== Description ==
=== Kokuzō Kitajima family genealogy ===
<div style = "overflow: auto;white-space:nowrap">
{{familytree/start|style = font-size:85%}}
{{familytree | border = 0|||||||01||||| 01 = Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto}}
{{familytree | border = 0| |||||||!}}
{{familytree | border = 0|||||||01||||| 01 = Takehina-no-Mikoto}}
{{familytree | border = 0| |||||||!}}
{{familytree | border = 0|||||||01||||| 01 = '''Isaga-no-Mikoto'''}}
{{familytree | border = 0| |||||||!}}
{{familytree | border = 0|||||||01||||| 01 = Tsusa-no-Mikoto}}
{{familytree | border = 0| |||||||!}}
{{familytree | border = 0|||||||01||||| 01 = {{ill|Kushitamasaki-no-Mikoto|ja|櫛𤭖前命}}}}
{{familytree | border = 0| |||||||!}}
{{familytree | border= 0|||||||01||||| 01 = {{ill|Kushitsuki-no-Mikoto|ja|櫛月命}}}}
{{familytree | border = 0| |||||||!}}
{{familytree | border = 0|||||||01||||| 01 = {{ill|Kushitamashima-Umi-no-Mikoto|ja|櫛𤭖嶋海命}}}}
{{familytree | border = 0| |||||||!}}
{{familytree | border = 0|||||||01||||| 01 = {{ill|Kushita-no-Mikoto|ja|櫛田命}}}}
{{familytree | border = 0| |||||||!}}
{{familytree | border = 0|||||||01||||| 01 = {{ill|Chiri-no-Mikoto|ja|知理命}}}}
{{familytree | border = 0| |||||||!}}
{{familytree | border = 0|||||||01||||| 01 = {{ill|Yomorosu-no-Mikoto|ja|世毛呂須命}}}}
{{familytree | border = 0| |||||||!}}
{{familytree | border = 0|||||||01||||| 01 = {{ill|Izumo-Furune|ja|出雲振根|lt=Ata-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=阿多命}}}}
{{familytree | border = 0| |||||||!}}
{{familytree | border = 0|||||||01||||| 01 = {{ill|Iineri|ja|飯入根|lt=Ihene-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=伊幣根命}}}}
{{familytree | border = 0| |||||||!}}
{{familytree | border = 0|||||||01||||| 01 = (following omitted)}}
{{familytree/end}}
</div>
</div>
== Examination ==
It has been argued that the Isaga in the deity name may be a word changed from Isasakaha / Isakaha, meaning "small river"{{Sfn|Shiga|1981|pp=427-433, 443, 456-457}}. Regarding the genealogy from Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto to Ihene-no-Mikoto (corresponding to the 1st through 12th generations in the ''Kokuzō Kitajima Keifu'') visible in the Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko genealogy, it has been argued that, since shrines enshrining Ame-no-Hohi-no-Mikoto in {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|出雲国}} were still listed as {{ill|Shikige-sha|ja|式外社}} at the time of compilation of the ''{{ill|Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}'' and only became {{ill|official shrines|ja|官社}} in a later era, these were newly incorporated as the {{ill|ancestral deity|ja|祖神}} of the Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko{{Sfn|Takashima|1995|pp=175-178}}.
== Shrines Enshrining Him ==
In Awata Hiroshi's ''Jingi Shiryō'', in addition to Kamu-Isaga Shrine (enshrined at Asuki Shrine, described below), the enshrined deities of Isaba Shrine (enshrined at Tsugari Shrine, Tōhayashimachi, {{ill|Izumo, Shimane|ja|出雲市|lt=Izumo City|lt_ja=出雲市}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|ja|島根県}}) and Isaga Shrine (Shussai, {{ill|Hikawa-chō|ja|斐川町}}, Izumo City) are also considered to be Isaga-no-Mikoto{{Sfn|Awata|1971|pp=457-458, 460-461, 463}}; however, Isaba Shrine states in its shrine tradition that the enshrined deity is {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美尊}}{{Sfn|Shiga|1981|pp=427-433, 443, 456-457}}, and Isaga Shrine currently enshrines {{ill|Abo-no-Ōkami|ja|阿菩大神}}, a deity that appears in the ''{{ill|Harima-no-Kuni Fudoki|ja|播磨国風土記}}''{{Sfn|Yoshioka|1983|p=543}}.
* Asuki Shrine ({{ill|Taisha-chō|ja|大社町}} {{ill|Yōkan-mura|ja|遙堪村|lt=Yōkan|lt_ja=遙堪}}, Izumo City, Shimane Prefecture) - merged {{Unbulleted list|Identified as the {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社}} Asuki Shrine, and the same shrine, the Shikinai-sha Kamu-Isaga Shrine, is enshrined there as well{{Sfn|Shiga|1981|pp=427-433, 443, 456-457}}{{Sfn|Awata|1971|pp=457-458, 460-461, 463}}. Among the shrines described as within the same shrine as Asuki Shrine in the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}," Kamu-Isaga Shrine is the only one whose {{ill|festival date|ja|祭日}} (November 14) is recorded in the ''{{ill|Tokusen Shinmyōchō|ja|特選神名牒}}'', and originally it is considered to have been an independent shrine{{Sfn|Shiga|1981|pp=427-433, 443, 456-457}}.}}
== Footnotes ==
{{脚注ヘルプ}}
=== Citations ===
<references />
== References ==
* {{Cite book|和書 |title=Jingi Shiryō |date=August 31, 1971 |publisher=Shibunkaku |ref={{SfnRef|Awata|1971}} |author=Awata Hiroshi (author), Awata Tsutomu (ed.) |edition=Reprint edition |ncid=BN06693594 |volume=Part 2}}
*{{Cite book|和書 |title=Shikinai-sha no Kenkyū |date=July 20, 1981 |publisher={{ill|Yūzankaku|ja|雄山閣}} |author=Shiga Takeshi |ref={{SfnRef|Shiga|1981}} |isbn=4-639-00083-9 |volume=Vol. 4, San'indō edition}}
* {{Cite book|和書 |title=Saitama Sōsho |date=June 21, 1929 |publisher=Sanmeisha |ref={{SfnRef|Shibata|Inamura|1929}} |editor={{ill|Shibata Tsunee|ja|柴田常恵}}, {{ill|Inamura Tangen|ja|稲村坦元}} |ncid=BN09959880 |volume=Vol. 3}}
* {{Cite book|和書 |title=Izumo Kokuzō Dentō Ryaku |date=April 1882 |publisher=Senge Taketoshi |ref={{SfnRef|Senge|1882}} |author=Senge Taketoshi (compiler) |ncid=BA55648765}}
* {{Cite book|和書 |title=Izumo Sekai to Kodai no San'in |date=February 1995 |publisher=Meicho Shuppan |ref={{SfnRef|Takashima|1995}} |author=Takashima Hiroshi |editor={{ill|Takioto Yoshiyuki|ja|瀧音能之}} |series=Kodai Ōken to Kōryū 7 |chapter=The Establishment and Development of the Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko |isbn=4-626-01505-0}}
* {{Cite journal|和書|author={{ill|Mitani Eiichi|ja|三谷栄一}}|date=1970-02-15|title=The Clan Background of Ancient Izumo — Transformation of Ōnamuchi Faith and the Izumo Civil War|journal=Kokugakuin Zasshi|volume=71|issue=2|publisher={{ill|Kokugakuin University|ja|國學院大學}}|ref={{SfnRef|Mitani|1970}}|crid=1520572357792178176|issn=0288-2051}}
* {{Cite book|和書 |title=Shikinai-sha Chōsa Hōkoku |date=February 1983 |publisher={{ill|Kōgakkan University|ja|皇學館大学}} Publishing Dept. |ref={{SfnRef|Yoshioka|1983}} |author=Yoshioka Shigeru |editor=Shikinai-sha Kenkyūkai |chapter=132 Isaga Shrine |volume=Vol. 20, San'indō 3 |ncid=BN00231541}}
== See also ==
* {{ill|Soshitsu-no-Mikoto|ja|襲髄命}} - considered the 14th (15th) generation in the Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko genealogy.
* {{ill|Kihisatsumi|ja|キヒサツミ}} - considered the same 15th (14th) generation as above.
== External links ==
* [https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/izumo/3e51691075516c87ae4ccc6d888be94b23d83bc4.html Asuki Shrine - Shrine Details | Shimane Prefecture Shrine Office]
* [https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/izumo/a5c84826230f62d8945debca61bf1d8f60f91244.html Tsugari Shrine - Shrine Details | Shimane Prefecture Shrine Office]
* [https://www.city.izumo.shimane.jp/www/contents/1610698250948/index.html Tsugari Shrine | Izumo City]
* [https://www.shimane-jinjacho.or.jp/izumo/02d8ec5b3f3040a08926279b988b5812d38c860a.html Isaga Shrine - Shrine Details | Shimane Prefecture Shrine Office]
* [https://kotobank.jp/word/%E4%BC%8A%E4%BD%90%E8%B3%80%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-3086496 Isaga Shrine: meaning and usage | Kotobank]
{{日本神話}}
{{神道 横}}
{{デフォルトソート:いさかのみこと}}
[[Category:日本の神]]
[[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]]
[[ja:伊佐我命]]
[[Category:Need translation]]
